title: GCaMP6f imaging using a mini-epifluorescence microscope (Mendonça et al 2024)
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"7bntf","text":"GCaMP6f imaging using a mini-epifluorescence microscope methods from Mendonça et al 2024. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"e3a46","text":"This procedure excludes surgical procedures that would occur prior to the imaging day. Please see 'virus injections and lens placement procedure' for those details. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5mj78","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1lknb","text":"Fluorescence images were acquired at 10 Hz and the LED power was set 40-60% with a gain of 4","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"eqgoj","text":"Mice are briefly anesthetized using a mixture of isoflurane and oxygen (1% isoflurane at 1L/min) and the mini-epifluorescence microscope was attached to the baseplate. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Once the baseplate (that is attached to the mouse) is attached to the mini-epifluorescence microscope, mice are recovered in their home cage for 15-20 minutes prior to starting imaging.
With the baseplate attached to the microscope, mice are them imaged during the FR4/1s task in 3 performance days. (see Single-limb fast FR4 operant task methods for details)
Image acquisition parameters were always set to the same parameters between sessions to be able to compare the activity recorded.
creator: Christine Weber-Schmidt
